# UC-22.10.11 · Business Associate Agreements — Expiry & Auto-Renewal Monitoring

## Description

Prevents lapsed BAAs for vendors that process ePHI by tracking expiry, auto-renew flags, and required amendment workflows.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes legal and sourcetypes ironclad:contract`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Nightly refresh from CLM API; (2) route CRITICAL to legal ops; (3) map vendor_id to technical assets for coverage attestation. Indexes required: `index=legal`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ironclad:contract``; Lookups: `baa_master`, `baa_master.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (CSV/HEC).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=legal` `sourcetype=ironclad:contract` or scheduled CSV `baa_master.csv` (`vendor_id`, `baa_end`, `auto_renew`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Nightly refresh from CLM API; (2) route CRITICAL to legal ops; (3) map vendor_id to technical assets for coverage attestation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup baa_master.csv
| eval days_to_end=round((strptime(baa_end,"%Y-%m-%d")-now())/86400,0)
| eval alert_tier=case(days_to_end<0,"EXPIRED",days_to_end<=30,"CRITICAL",days_to_end<=90,"WARN",1=1,"OK")
| where alert_tier!="OK"
| table vendor_id, vendor_name, baa_end, auto_renew, alert_tier, data_categories
| sort days_to_end
```

Understanding this SPL

**Business Associate Agreements — Expiry & Auto-Renewal Monitoring (§164.308(b)(1))** — Prevents lapsed BAAs for vendors that process ePHI by tracking expiry, auto-renew flags, and required amendment workflows.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=legal` `sourcetype=ironclad:contract` or scheduled CSV `baa_master.csv` (`vendor_id`, `baa_end`, `auto_renew`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (CSV/HEC). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_to_end** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **alert_tier** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where alert_tier!="OK"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: alert_tier, auto_renew, baa_end, data_categories, days_to_end, vendor_id, vendor_name.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (expired count), Table (upcoming renewals), Timeline (signature dates).

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup baa_master.csv
| eval days_to_end=round((strptime(baa_end,"%Y-%m-%d")-now())/86400,0)
| eval alert_tier=case(days_to_end<0,"EXPIRED",days_to_end<=30,"CRITICAL",days_to_end<=90,"WARN",1=1,"OK")
| where alert_tier!="OK"
| table vendor_id, vendor_name, baa_end, auto_renew, alert_tier, data_categories
| sort days_to_end
```

## Visualization

Single value (expired count), Table (upcoming renewals), Timeline (signature dates). Key fields: `alert_tier`, `auto_renew`, `baa_end`, `data_categories`, `days_to_end`, `vendor_id`.
